evidence: We expect spending in technology and infrastructure will increase over time...including to support our artificial intelligence and machine learning initiatives
shortened useful lives of servers and networking equipment from six years to five years due to the increased pace of technology development, particularly in the area of artificial intelligence
effect of this change...was an increase in depreciation and amortization expense of $1.4 billion and a reduction in net income of $1.0 billion
AWS, which offers a broad set of on-demand technology services, including compute, storage, database, analytics, artificial intelligence and machine learning, and other services
We are also investing in initiatives to build and deploy innovative and efficient software and electronic devices as well as other initiatives including the development of a satellite network
We further expect to continue making additional investments in our artificial intelligence initiatives

we rely on a limited group of suppliers for semiconductor products, including products related to artificial intelligence infrastructure such as graphics processing units. Constraints on the availability...could adversely affect our ability to develop and operate artificial intelligence technologies

our and our customers development and use of artificial intelligence may result in increased claims of infringement or other claims, including those based on unauthorized use of third-party technology or content
security incidents...may fail to operate as intended or be circumvented, including by use of developing technologies such as artificial intelligence

capex_mentions.quote: increase in depreciation and amortization expense of $1.4 billion and a reduction in net income of $1.0 billion
capex_mentions.context: Effect of shortening useful lives of servers/networking equipment from 6 to 5 years due to AI-driven technology pace, primarily impacting AWS segment, FY2025

capex_mentions.quote: Depreciation and amortization expense on property and equipment was $30.2 billion, $32.1 billion, and $41.9 billion
capex_mentions.context: D&A on property and equipment for 2023, 2024, 2025 respectively; includes data center and server infrastructure supporting AWS and AI initiatives

capex_mentions.quote: Property and equipment acquired but not yet paid...were $16.8 billion and $27.0 billion as of December 31, 2024 and 2025
capex_mentions.context: Unpaid capex commitments year-end 2024 and 2025, covering data center and infrastructure build-out

limitations: No explicit multi-year AI-specific capex dollar commitment is disclosed in the excerpts; the $1.4B figure relates to an accounting estimate change (useful life), not a forward capex pledge, limiting intensity score to 4 rather than 5
AI narrative concreteness is limited by the excerpts: AWS AI/ML services are named but no specific deployed AI product metrics or quantified business outcomes appear in the provided text

Risk disclosure excerpts are present and substantive; score reflects company-specific risk mechanisms (GPU supply concentration, IP infringement from AI use, cybersecurity circumvention via AI) but no quantified risk exposure or named governance body is disclosed in these excerpts
